data.title: DeFi Security Developments Highlight Mixed Outcomes Amid Market Uncertainty
data.description: Recent developments in decentralized finance (DeFi) security have shown a blend of successful hack recoveries and persistent vulnerabilities. According to NS3.AI, prominent firms are under fire for security shortcomings, while new funding commitments are being made to bolster Ethereum's infrastructure. Additionally, major cryptocurrency exchanges remain under scrutiny as market uncertainty continues in the wake of significant crashes.

Crypto and TradFi will continue to converge on multiple metrics, claims the latest report by Eliptic.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/crypto-and-tradfi-convergence-to-accelerate-in-2026/

data.title: Crypto Futures Market Experiences Significant Liquidations Amid Volatility
data.description: In the past hour, major exchanges have witnessed futures liquidations amounting to $108 million. According to NS3.AI, the past 24 hours have seen a surge in liquidations, reaching a total of $1.42 billion. This substantial activity highlights the increased volatility currently affecting the crypto futures market.

data.title: Analyst Predicts End of Precious Metals Boom
data.description:
The era of unprecedented growth in precious metals may have concluded, according to ChainCatcher. Analyst Adam Button from investinglive suggests that the market for precious metals has reached its peak.

Button expressed skepticism about gold prices falling below $4,000, noting that if such a drop occurs, it might attract well-capitalized investors. However, he advises caution and refrains from making bold predictions at this time. For current holders of gold, the situation presents a challenging decision, as market conditions could either improve rapidly or experience significant volatility. Button warns against making panic-driven decisions during late Friday trading sessions.

Regarding the possibility of a further sell-off akin to "Black Monday," Button believes it is unlikely unless triggered by factors beyond the Iranian agreement.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Mester Anticipates Economic Growth Above Trend Level
data.description: The Federal Reserve's Mester forecasts that the economy will continue to grow at a rate above the trend level, driven by credit conditions and fiscal policy. According to ChainCatcher, this outlook suggests sustained economic momentum influenced by these factors.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Mussailem Predicts Inflation Trends
data.description: Federal Reserve official Mussailem has projected that inflation will decrease to around 2%. According to Jin10, Mussailem also expressed concerns that inflation might remain above 2% for an extended period. He cautioned that further interest rate cuts could exacerbate inflation expectations.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Mester: Further Rate Cuts Unnecessary as Current Policy Is Neutral
data.description: Federal Reserve official Loretta Mester stated that further interest rate cuts are not advisable, as the current monetary policy is already neutral. According to Jin10, Mester emphasized that the economy does not require additional stimulus at this time. She noted that risks are generally balanced and that further rate reductions would only be necessary if there is a deterioration in the labor market or a decline in inflation.

data.title: Avalanche Breaks Key Support as Downside Structure Reasserts Control
data.description:
Avalanche is trading near $10. 95 at the time of writing, following a decisive breakdown that has pushed price below a long-defended support zone. After several failed rebound attempts, the market has shifted into a more vulnerable structural position, with sellers regaining control
source: https://www.ethnews.com/avalanche-breaks-key-support-as-downside-structure-reasserts-control/

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Drops Below 82,000 USDT with a 2.49% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Jan 30, 2026, 18:23 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has dropped below 82,000 USDT and is now trading at 81,935 USDT, with a narrowed 2.49% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Ethereum Address Poisoning Attack Leads to $12.3 Million Theft
data.description: A sophisticated address poisoning attack has led to the theft of $12.3 million in Ethereum (ETH). According to NS3.AI, the victim intended to transfer funds to a legitimate address but was tricked into sending them to a malicious, similar-looking address. This incident underscores the persistent security challenges in cryptocurrency transactions, as reported by blockchain analytics firm Cyvers Alerts.

data.description: On Jan 30, 2026, 18:13 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Ethereum has dropped below 2,700 USDT and is now trading at 2,699.610107 USDT, with a narrowed 4.63% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Ethereum Re-Engages Key Demand Zone After Loss of $2,800
data.description:
Ethereum is trading near $2,718 on the ETH/USDT 4-hour chart, following a sharp downside move that has pushed price back into a structurally important demand zone. After failing to sustain higher levels earlier in the week, ETH has entered a compression phase, with volatility cooling but directional clarity still absent.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/ethereum-re-engages-key-demand-zone-after-loss-of-2800/

data.title: Solana Pressures Key Support as Structure Weakens Below $120
data.description:
Solana is trading near $116. 9 at the time of writing, following a sharp sell-off that has pushed price back toward a critical structural area. The market is attempting to stabilize after a steep downside impulse, but current positioning suggests the rebound remains fragile and corrective rather than impulsive
source: https://www.ethnews.com/solana-pressures-key-support-as-structure-weakens-below-120/

data.title: Visa and Mastercard Question Stablecoin Adoption for Daily Transactions
data.description: Visa and Mastercard have raised doubts about the widespread use of stablecoins for everyday payments in developed markets. According to NS3.AI, the companies argue that while stablecoins offer the potential for quicker and more cost-effective transactions, existing payment infrastructures already meet consumer demands efficiently. As a result, these leading payment networks are hesitant to incorporate stablecoins into their systems at this time.
